title: Is Graph Bipartite
coreIdea: Determine if a graph's nodes can be divided into two groups such that no edge connects nodes within the same group, by attempting to 2-color the graph via BFS/DFS; a conflict (adjacent nodes forced to the same color) means the graph is not bipartite.
recognitionCues: asks if a graph can be divided into two independent sets / is bipartite
graph may be disconnected, requiring checks from multiple components
approach: Maintain a colors array (uncolored initially). For each uncolored node, start BFS/DFS assigning it color 0. For each neighbor, if uncolored, assign the opposite color and continue; if already colored, check it differs from the current node's color â€” if not, the graph is not bipartite. Repeat for all disconnected components.
